design_philosophy.core_loop: Combat rounds between two fighters using special moves and combos; defeat opponent to advance through tournament bracket
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Parody and irreverence as primary design axis—subverting fighting game tropes through absurdist character design and clay animation aesthetic
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Engage in stylized, humorous 1v1 combat with grotesque, whimsical clay-sculpted opponents in a tournament setting
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: ClayFighter embraces visual comedy and parody over mechanical depth or serious competitive play. It satirizes Street Fighter and Mortal Kombat through character design (Bad Mr. Frosty, Blob, Bonker the clown) rather than gameplay innovation. The clay animation aesthetic is intentionally non-realistic, prioritizing charm and spectacle over fidelity—a deliberate stance against the emerging 3D fighter trend of the mid-1990s.

difficulty.checkpointing: Round-based progression; tournament bracket advances upon successive round victories

progression.unlock_mechanism: Character roster unlocked through gameplay; tournament progression gates access to new opponents
replayability_hooks: Multiple playable characters with distinct movesets
Tournament bracket structure encourages multiple playthroughs with different characters
Recurring character roster across series iterations suggests roster expansion as motivation for replay

world_building_techniques: Character design (archetypal parodies: snowman, blob, clown, scarecrow) establishes tone through visual identity
Clay animation aesthetic creates cohesive, internally consistent absurdist world
Parody of fighting game conventions (implicit comparison to Street Fighter, Mortal Kombat) builds context through genre reference

boss_design.philosophy: Boss characters function as tournament opponents with no special mechanical distinction; difficulty progression through opponent selection rather than escalating boss mechanics
notable_bosses: Bonker (bad clown—appears in all five series games)
Ickybod Clay (scarecrow ghost with pumpkin head)

faction_indicators: Character archetypes suggest minimal faction framework (individual clay creatures rather than ideological camps)
environmental_storytelling: Tournament setting implies competitive framework with implied victory conditions and ranking systems
Recurring character appearances across game iterations suggest persistent clay-world continuity
